title: Boulder Co Vlog 4/8/18: Roller Rode 27min, Swam 1524M, Ran 5.5Mi

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs the previous day's triathlon-style training in Boulder, CO on April 8, 2018. He recounts sleeping most of the day after a Unisom pill backfired, dealing with a flat tire and excruciating knee pain, then pushing through a swim of ~1524 meters (100 pool lengths), a 27-minute bike ride, and a 5.5-mile run — all interrupted repeatedly by severe stomach bloating that required Pepto-Bismol to resolve. He closes with notes on his Garmin troubleshooting, his habit of exercising until groggy, and a plan to start doing book/history talks once he runs out of weed.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Exercise until groggy — same rule he used when working, no reason to stop before that
Plans to start recording book and history discussions when he runs out of weed, framing it as a natural transition

transcript.text: Hello, Levin and Bald V-Log for April 8.
Currently, it is April 9th at 1.37 in the morning, Monday.
So yesterday, I took a sleeping pill, a Unisom, but I didn't do anything.
It's just, I was up all night.
And I could have gotten up and worked out at 5 in the morning, but I was too tired.
And I didn't feel like I could ride.
I didn't want to swim or run.
That was my first time when I was that tired.
And that's when the Unisom kicked in, so I slept like a baby all day.
And then got up at like 5 or something and got crickets.
And I had a gyro, which was nasty.
And then came home, noticed that my car was flat, so I took the tire to the place to have that fixed.
And then came back and, yeah, but I noticed when I went to the store, my knee was excruciating pain, just moving it.
So I definitely wanted to do running or swimming.
So I came back and went swimming.
I couldn't find my swimsuit, so tomorrow I'm going to buy another swimsuit.
I also need to figure out a way to make it so that the nose plug doesn't slip off or figure out how to do the flip kicks without having a nose plug.
But I want to be able to do the snorkel.
I think you need a nose plug for that.
So I'll go in and see if I can figure that out tomorrow.
I was swimming at the beginning with a snorkel, then a nose plug fell out.
So I started swimming normally and managed to do 100 lengths, which is about 1,500 meters.
That's about how much they swim in the, I think something like that, in the Olympics, almost a mile.
So 100 lengths of my 50-foot pool.
Before, my stomach just bloated really big and became excruciatingly painful.
So I left and started riding my bike, and I managed to ride 27 minutes.
My Garmin, I'm not going to upload it because my Garmin wouldn't connect to the heart rate or the speedometer.
So I'll try to do the same thing, I guess, tomorrow, what the Garmin told me the other day,
Because this happened the other day, where you take the battery out and you have to touch the positive and negative
With a piece of metal at the same time, like a coin, and then put it back in and then see.
So I'll do that.
But I made it about 27 minutes until my stomach continued to bloat, and it got really big and was way too painful.
So I stopped, went to the store, got double strength, Pepto-Bismol, and some other food.
And took that, and then my stomach was fine to go running.
So I ran, I think, about 5 1⁄2 miles.
I ran, made it about 2 1⁄2 miles before the thing timed out.
And so I think to keep it going, you just do a bunch of 5K runs.
So I did another 5K, so I totaled maybe 5 1⁄2 miles.
And I swam like a mile.
And then, so I haven't ridden again.
I was thinking maybe I'll ride now, but it's 1.40 in the morning.
We'll see.
I think a good rule of thumb for me now is just to exercise until I get groggy.
Because that's what I was doing before when I was working on my website.
I just work on the website until they get groggy.
So I should do the same thing.
Otherwise, I'll just be laying there, you know.
Whether it's riding or stretching.
I think I'll stretch now.
I haven't stretched once since I started exercising.
So, but yeah, I'm sweating a lot with the riding and the running.
Probably swimming, too.
So, yeah, I'm going to get fit.
It's exciting.
Um, yeah, just listening to music.
I haven't started doing any, uh, audiobook listening.
I'm still smoking weed all the time.
When I run out of weed, I think that's when I'll, I'll probably decide that's the time
That I'll do the, uh, audiobooks talking about it.
That'll be interesting.
And the test, history test.
So, that's what I did yesterday.
Keeping up with it.
